untranslatable_reason: Signal requires pricing of derivatives (calls, puts, synthetic fair values using put-call parity: stock - put = call_fair_value). This requires access to options chain data with strike prices, implied volatility, and time-to-expiration—none of which are available in the ALLOWED_INDICATORS list (which only contains equity price/volume/technical indicators). The mispricing detection logic (call_price vs synthetic_fair_value) cannot be expressed with single-leg price/indicator comparisons alone.
translation_notes: Source example shows: stock=100, put=4.00, synthetic_call_fair_value=5.00, actual_call=4.25 (underpriced by 0.75). Reverse conversion = short call + long put + short stock. Generic placeholder conditions shown using close price as proxy, but this does NOT capture the true put-call parity relationship required. A proper implementation would need options Greeks and multi-leg synthetic pricing.
